channel.title: Comments for page "On the Outskirts"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "On the Outskirts"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that I accidentally did a page revert on some grammar corrections, since the version comparison displayed the revisions weirdly. The changes stand, mea culpa.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has got to be one of my favorite tales. It gives so much insight into what life in the Foundation is like, which is something I have contemplated for a long time. I’m looking forward to seeing a lot more like this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked your take on an anomaly which, in the grand scheme of things, is just another scip. The guy was characterized in an interesting and compelling way, and I really liked his outsider's analysis to many of the things we on the site are very used to. There were a few places where the tone was not as tight as it could be, but overall it was a very enjoyable read, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure why, but the idea of a bunch of Sarkics just rolling over what basically amounts to normal civilians, only to get struck by lightning is hilarious to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never seen anything like this before, and now I have to wonder why. I love this dude's affable "regular guy" attitude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So glad to see more excellent works for my favorite canon. <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a tale about my own SCP-3310, but more importantly the relationship of somebody who's tangentially related to the Foundation to the Foundation. I've always thought that there must be a lot of people who would know about the Foundation but not have much interaction with them - even if they're on the payroll and involved in the containment of an anomaly, a guard for a random anomaly in the middle of a small town probably doesn't know that much about the Foundation at large.

Thanks to Hatsune Miku and DrConnors for looking this over.
